excerpt: the soul generates desires in endless waves. When a person fails to exercise intellect, the soul becomes a tempestuous sea that carries him toward destruction
source_location_hint: discussions of nafs and intellectual perfection

excerpt: After death, only the deeds remain of a person: the good and the evil recorded in his account. When the person is placed in the grave and experiences its punishment, profound regret seizes him
source_location_hint: eschatological teachings on deeds and the grave

excerpt: When you recite the verses of mercy, your heart yearns for the blessings of Allah; when you recite the verses of punishment, your body trembles with fear of Him
source_location_hint: supplications regarding Qur'an and the heart

excerpt: One who fasts for thirty days but neglects the fiṭra squanders his month of discipline; the pilgrim who offers no sacrificial animal renders the entire pilgrimage void
source_location_hint: laws of fasting, hajj, and ritual completion

excerpt: If that life does not culminate in loyalty to ʿAlī and his descendants—in recognition of their rightful authority—then all his striving is rendered hollow
source_location_hint: esoteric interpretation of wilāya and culmination of faith

excerpt: God has created numerous noble beings; among all creation, humanity alone has been granted the title of the noblest of creatures. This pre-eminence derives from the intellect
source_location_hint: philosophical anthropology and the excellence of intellect

excerpt: The beginning of wrath is madness, and its end is regret; whoever does not regret at the end remains mad. The best of you is one who becomes angry yet hastens to composure
source_location_hint: sayings of ʿAli on anger and temperance

excerpt: When Cain became envious of Abel, he murdered him—though the killing profited him nothing and cost him damnation in the Hereafter
source_location_hint: exegesis of the Cain and Abel narrative and the vice of envy

excerpt: The greedy person becomes shameless, stretching out his hand before people without regard for his own honor, caring only to satisfy his appetite
source_location_hint: moral psychology of shame and avarice

excerpt: God has said that backbiting is like consuming the flesh of one's dead brother. The flesh of the dead is already forbidden, and naturally abhorrent to man
source_location_hint: jurisprudential treatment of backbiting and forbidden speech

excerpt: The person who pursues the world and makes it his end-goal shall receive three things in return: a heart seized by wrath, humiliation among people, and entanglement in calamities
source_location_hint: teachings on the three types of hearts and their ends

excerpt: The person whose effort is directed toward knowledge of his Lord strives toward something beyond himself—toward the perfection of a being greater than his own. For this reason, his virtue increases immeasurably in the sight of God
source_location_hint: discussion of three types of seekers and their stations

excerpt: Striving in the way of God means exertion necessary to attain knowledge of God's Oneness and recognition of Him, to understand the cosmic order, to know who stands in what position
source_location_hint: interpretation of jihād as spiritual struggle and cosmic knowledge

excerpt: Such a person remains loyal to the Imam and his daʿwa, fulfilling both external and inward obligations entrusted to him
source_location_hint: teachings on loyalty to the Imam and the daʿwa system

excerpt: The dog guarding the Companions of the Cave exemplifies unwavering devotion to his master. This beast follows every command without hesitation, no matter what trials he faces
source_location_hint: allegorical exegesis of Sura al-Kahf and exemplary loyalty

excerpt: Yes, every meeting of friends must end in parting. All that we have before death is fleeting and small. In my longing for Fatima after the loss of Ahmad, I have found proof that no companion can endure forever
source_location_hint: elegies and meditations on death and separation

excerpt: Before this grave, with these hands, I placed Muhammad to rest. Today, with these same hands, I say farewell to Fatima. Every friend, however close, must one day part
source_location_hint: ʿAli's elegies and laments at the burial of Fatima
